name: Tafalla and Olite Royal Palace Loop
name_original: Tafalla - Palacio Real de Olite – variante circular de la Vuelta a la Nava (BTT Zona Media)
description: Mountain bike the Tafalla - Palacio Real de Olite route, a moderate 16.7-mile loop through orchards and forests to a medieval castle.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Tafalla offers varied terrain across valleys, hills, and riverine landscapes. The region features rolling hills and the Río Cidacos, providing diverse options for cyclists. Areas like Valdorba and the Guerinda Mountain Range present more demanding routes with significant elevation changes. Monte Plano also offers elevated views accessible via the trail network. Best mountain bike trails around Tafalla The most popular mountain bike trail is Tafalla and Olite Royal Palace Loop, a 16.7 miles (26.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 59 minutes to complete. This route takes cyclists through orchards and forests to the medieval castle in Olite. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Hermitage of Santa Zita – Molino de Olleta loop from Tafalla, a moderate 25.9 miles (41.7 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain, including sections of the Guerinda Mountain Range. Local mountain bikers also love the Beratxa – Tafalla loop from Tafalla, a 23.0 miles (37.0 km) trail leading through rolling hills and valleys, often completed in about 3 hours 8 minutes. Mountain biking around Tafalla is defined by valleys, hills, and riverine landscapes, with options for different ability levels. The network includes both paved and unpaved sections, catering to various fitness levels. The routes in Tafalla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Tafalla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Tafalla?
A: There are over 25 mountain bike trails around Tafalla, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The region's network spans approximately 288 miles (463 km) in total.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Tafalla?
A: Yes, Tafalla offers several easy mountain bike trails, with 5 routes specifically categorized as easy. The region's diverse terrain includes options suitable for all skill levels, including family-friendly routes like the &lt;em&gt;14-Tafalla-Pueyo route&lt;/em&gt; along the Cidacos River, which is primarily cross-country.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Tafalla?
A: The mountain bike trails around Tafalla feature varied terrain, including rolling hills, valleys, and riverine landscapes along the Río Cidacos. You'll encounter both paved and unpaved sections, with some routes leading through orchards and forests, and others offering more technical segments in areas like Valdorba and the Guerinda Mountain Range.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Tafalla?
A: Yes, Tafalla has challenging routes for experienced mountain bikers. While 20 routes are moderate, there are 2 difficult trails available. Routes like the &lt;em&gt;San Martin de Unx – Hermitage of San José de Tafalla loop&lt;/em&gt; are known for demanding very good fitness and include unpaved sections. The Guerinda Mountain Range also features longer routes with significant ascents.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Tafalla?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Tafalla are circular. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988407444/tafalla-and-olite-royal-palace-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tafalla and Olite Royal Palace Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Zita – Molino de Olleta loop from Tafalla&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38158602" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beratxa – Tafalla loop from Tafalla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Tafalla?
A: Many trails integrate historical and cultural sites. You can cycle to the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2704481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Palace of Olite&lt;/a&gt;, explore the walled town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5322022" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artajona&lt;/a&gt;, or pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5303911" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;town of Ujué&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7013438" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6250557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María la Real&lt;/a&gt; are also notable religious buildings in the area.

Q: Are there any natural viewpoints or scenic spots along the trails?
A: Yes, the region offers several scenic spots. Monte Plano provides elevated views of the surrounding area, including the Moncayo and the Pyrenees on clear days. The Valdorba area, featuring the Montes Valdorba, also offers scenic vistas. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5532197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sunk Summit&lt;/a&gt; is listed as a viewpoint.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Tafalla?
A: The mountain bike trails in Tafalla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the well-maintained network, and the integration of natural beauty with historical sites like the Royal Palace of Olite.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Tafalla?
A: Tafalla's diverse trails are regularly updated and maintained, making them accessible for much of the year. While specific seasonal recommendations aren't provided, the region's mix of valleys, hills, and riverine landscapes suggests pleasant riding conditions during spring and autumn, avoiding the peak heat of summer or potential winter conditions.

Q: Are there any natural monuments or unique natural features to explore?
A: Yes, Tafalla's natural environment includes several unique features. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6251952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancient Olive Tree of Olite&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7521496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quejigo of Garínoain (Quercus faginea)&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7183819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;AltoDelTxaparro summit&lt;/a&gt;. The Laguna del Juncal is also an emblematic natural lagoon in the area.

Q: How long are the mountain bike trails around Tafalla?
A: The mountain bike trails around Tafalla vary significantly in length. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988407444/tafalla-and-olite-royal-palace-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tafalla and Olite Royal Palace Loop&lt;/a&gt; is 16.7 miles (26.9 km) long, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Zita – Molino de Olleta loop from Tafalla&lt;/a&gt; extends to 25.9 miles (41.7 km).
tips: Very cool path that runs along the signposted itinerary of Monte Plano visiting some iconic places in the area among a very typical landscape of the Navarrese interior.

The Palace of the Kings of Navarre of Olite or Royal Palace of Olite is a castle-palace in the town of Olite, in Navarre, Spain. It was one of the seats of the Court of the Kingdom of Navarre, since the reign of Charles III "the Noble" until its conquest by Castile (1512).
Nice and quiet place next to the town of Tafalla, a strategic place to relax and rest for a while next to the hermitage of San José.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This mountain biking route is approximately 26.9 km long and typically takes around 2 hours to complete, depending on your pace and any stops you make to explore points of interest.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is rated as moderate difficulty. While it's part of the BTT Zona Media network which offers routes for all levels, some sections, particularly within the Monteplano area, can be winding and technical. It's generally accessible to a broader range of riders but might challenge absolute beginners.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail in Tafalla or Olite?
faqs.answer: The route connects the towns of Tafalla and Olite. You can likely find parking in either town to begin your ride. For specific starting points, it's advisable to check local parking options in Tafalla, especially near the orchards along the Cidacos river, or in Olite, close to the historic center.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, traversing agricultural land, orchards, and sections through pine and oak forests, particularly in the Monte Plano Trail - Tafalla area. You'll encounter both 'senderos' (singletracks/trails) and 'tramos de camino' (track sections), with some parts being winding and technical.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers several highlights. You'll pass through the Monte Plano Trail - Tafalla, known for its mountain biking. A major highlight is the medieval town of Olite, home to the magnificent Royal Palace of Olite. You'll also pass by the Hermitage of San José de Tafalla.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the agricultural and forested landscapes, spring and autumn are generally ideal for mountain biking in this region, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be hot, while winter might bring colder, potentially wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Is the Tafalla - Palacio Real de Olite mountain biking route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many outdoor trails in Spain are dog-friendly, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas, near livestock, or in populated sections of Tafalla and Olite. Be mindful of local regulations and ensure your dog is well-behaved around other trail users.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, no specific permits or entrance fees are required to ride the mountain biking trails within the BTT Zona Media network. However, if you plan to enter the Royal Palace of Olite, there will be an admission fee.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in the BTT Zona Media network?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is a circular variant of the 'Vuelta a la Nava' (Route 11) within the extensive BTT Zona Media network. It also incorporates sections of official trails such as Sendero Histórico: Navarra, Paseo de la República, and various local streets like Calle Martinez de Espronceda and Rúa Romana.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trail?
faqs.answer: Both Tafalla and Olite are well-established towns with amenities. You'll find various cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in both locations, making them convenient spots for pre- or post-ride refreshments and overnight stays.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this route?
faqs.answer: For this moderate mountain biking route, essential items include a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, water, snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, multi-tool), and navigation tools. Sun protection is also advisable, especially during warmer months.

faqs.question: What's an insider tip for experiencing the Royal Palace of Olite during the ride?
faqs.answer: It is highly recommended to allocate extra time to deviate from the main route and explore the old town of Olite and the magnificent Royal Palace of Olite. This 15th-century Gothic castle is considered one of Europe's best examples of civil Gothic architecture and Navarra's most important medieval monument, offering impressive views and a rich historical experience.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of San José de Tafalla
